92	    def __init__(
93	        self,
94	        tokenizer_manager: TokenizerManager,
95	        template_manager: TemplateManager,
96	    ):
97	        super().__init__(tokenizer_manager)
98	        self.template_manager = template_manager
99	        self.tool_call_parser = self.tokenizer_manager.server_args.tool_call_parser
100	        self.reasoning_parser = self.tokenizer_manager.server_args.reasoning_parser
101	
102	        # Get default sampling parameters from model's generation config
103	        self.default_sampling_params = (
104	            self.tokenizer_manager.model_config.get_default_sampling_params()
105	        )
106	        if (
107	            self.default_sampling_params
108	            and not OpenAIServingChat._default_sampling_params_logged
109	        ):
110	            logger.info(
111	                f"Using default chat sampling params from model generation config: {self.default_sampling_params}",
112	            )
113	            OpenAIServingChat._default_sampling_params_logged = True
114	
115	        # Check if the model is a GPT-OSS model
116	        self.is_gpt_oss = (
117	            hasattr(self.tokenizer_manager.model_config, "hf_config")
118	            and hasattr(self.tokenizer_manager.model_config.hf_config, "model_type")
119	            and self.tokenizer_manager.model_config.hf_config.model_type == "gpt_oss"
120	        )
121	
122	        self.use_dpsk_v32_encoding = self._use_dpsk_v32_encoding()
123	
124	    def _handle_last_assistant_message(
125	        self,
126	        messages: List[Dict[str, Any]],
127	        request: ChatCompletionRequest,
128	    ) -> tuple[List[Dict[str, Any]], Optional[str]]:
129	        """
130	        Handle continue_final_message feature: separate final assistant message.
131	
132	        If continue_final_message is enabled and the last message is from assistant,
133	        extract its content and remove it from the message list.
134	        If continue_final_message is False and the last message is from assistant,
135	        convert it to a user message to ensure the last message is always from user.
136	
137	        Only processes text-based content (strings), ignoring multimodal content (lists).
138	
139	        Args:
140	            messages: List of message dictionaries
141	            request: ChatCompletionRequest with continue_final_message flag
142	
143	        Returns:
144	            Tuple of (processed_messages, assistant_prefix)
145	            - processed_messages: Messages with last assistant message handled appropriately
146	            - assistant_prefix: Content of the last assistant message (string only), or None
147	        """
148	        assistant_prefix = None
149	        if messages and messages[-1].get("role") == "assistant":
150	            last_content = messages[-1].get("content")
151	            # Only process string content, ignore multimodal content (lists)
152	            if isinstance(last_content, str):
153	                if request.continue_final_message:
154	                    # Extract content and remove the assistant message
155	                    assistant_prefix = last_content
156	                    messages = messages[:-1]
157	                else:
158	                    # Convert the last assistant message to user message
159	                    messages[-1] = {"role": "user", "content": last_content}
160	        return messages, assistant_prefix
161	
162	    def _append_assistant_prefix_to_prompt_ids(
163	        self, prompt_ids: List[int], assistant_prefix: str
164	    ) -> List[int]:
165	        """
166	        Append assistant prefix to prompt_ids.
167	
168	        Args:
169	            prompt_ids: Current prompt token IDs
170	            assistant_prefix: Assistant message content to append
171	
172	        Returns:
173	            Updated prompt_ids with assistant prefix appended
174	        """
175	        encoded = self.tokenizer_manager.tokenizer.encode(assistant_prefix)
176	        if encoded and encoded[0] == self.tokenizer_manager.tokenizer.bos_token_id:
177	            encoded = encoded[1:]
178	        return prompt_ids + encoded
179	
180	    def _use_dpsk_v32_encoding(self) -> bool:
181	        has_chat_template = (
182	            self.tokenizer_manager.tokenizer is not None
183	            and self.tokenizer_manager.tokenizer.chat_template is not None
184	        )
185	        architectures = self.tokenizer_manager.model_config.hf_config.architectures
186	        is_dpsk_v32 = "DeepseekV3" in architectures[0] if architectures else False
187	        return not has_chat_template and is_dpsk_v32

189	    def _request_id_prefix(self) -> str:
190	        return "chatcmpl-"
191	
192	    def _validate_request(self, request: ChatCompletionRequest) -> Optional[str]:
193	        """Validate that the input is valid."""
194	        if not request.messages:
195	            return "Messages cannot be empty."
196	
197	        if (
198	            isinstance(request.tool_choice, str)
199	            and request.tool_choice.lower() == "required"
200	            and not request.tools
201	        ):
202	            return "Tools cannot be empty if tool choice is set to required."
203	
204	        if request.tool_choice is not None and not isinstance(request.tool_choice, str):
205	            if not request.tools:
206	                return "Tools cannot be empty if tool choice is set to a specific tool."
207	            tool_name = request.tool_choice.function.name
208	            tool_exists = any(tool.function.name == tool_name for tool in request.tools)
209	            if not tool_exists:
210	                return f"Tool '{tool_name}' not found in tools list."
211	
212	        # Validate tool definitions
213	        for i, tool in enumerate(request.tools or []):
214	            if tool.function.parameters is None:
215	                continue
216	            try:
217	                Draft202012Validator.check_schema(tool.function.parameters)
218	            except SchemaError as e:
219	                return f"Tool {i} function has invalid 'parameters' schema: {str(e)}"
220	
221	        max_output_tokens = request.max_completion_tokens or request.max_tokens
222	        server_context_length = self.tokenizer_manager.server_args.context_length
223	        if (
224	            max_output_tokens
225	            and server_context_length
226	            and max_output_tokens > server_context_length
227	        ) and not self.tokenizer_manager.server_args.allow_auto_truncate:
228	            return (
229	                f"max_completion_tokens is too large: {max_output_tokens}."
230	                f"This model supports at most {server_context_length} completion tokens."
231	            )
232	
233	        if request.response_format and request.response_format.type == "json_schema":
234	            schema = getattr(request.response_format.json_schema, "schema_", None)
235	            if schema is None:
236	                return "schema_ is required for json_schema response format request."
237	
238	        return None
239	
240	    def _convert_to_internal_request(
241	        self,
242	        request: ChatCompletionRequest,
243	        raw_request: Request = None,
244	    ) -> tuple[GenerateReqInput, ChatCompletionRequest]:
245	        reasoning_effort = (
246	            request.chat_template_kwargs.pop("reasoning_effort", None)
247	            if request.chat_template_kwargs
248	            else None
249	        )
250	        if reasoning_effort is not None:
251	            request.reasoning_effort = reasoning_effort
252	
253	        """Convert OpenAI chat completion request to internal format"""
254	        is_multimodal = self.tokenizer_manager.model_config.is_multimodal
255	
256	        # Process messages and apply chat template
257	        processed_messages = self._process_messages(request, is_multimodal)
258	
259	        # Build sampling parameters
260	        sampling_params = request.to_sampling_params(
261	            stop=processed_messages.stop,
262	            model_generation_config=self.default_sampling_params,
263	            tool_call_constraint=processed_messages.tool_call_constraint,
264	        )
265	
266	        # Handle single vs multiple requests
267	        if is_multimodal:
268	            prompt_kwargs = {"text": processed_messages.prompt}
269	        else:
270	            if isinstance(processed_messages.prompt_ids, str):
271	                prompt_kwargs = {"text": processed_messages.prompt_ids}
272	            else:
273	                prompt_kwargs = {"input_ids": processed_messages.prompt_ids}
274	
275	        # Extract custom labels from raw request headers
276	        custom_labels = self.extract_custom_labels(raw_request)
277	
278	        # Resolve LoRA adapter from model parameter or explicit lora_path
279	        lora_path = self._resolve_lora_path(request.model, request.lora_path)
280	        img_max_dynamic_patch, vid_max_dynamic_patch = _extract_max_dynamic_patch(
281	            request
282	        )
283	        adapted_request = GenerateReqInput(
284	            **prompt_kwargs,
285	            image_data=processed_messages.image_data,
286	            video_data=processed_messages.video_data,
287	            audio_data=processed_messages.audio_data,
288	            sampling_params=sampling_params,
289	            return_logprob=request.logprobs,
290	            logprob_start_len=-1,
291	            top_logprobs_num=request.top_logprobs or 0,
292	            stream=request.stream,
293	            return_text_in_logprobs=True,
294	            modalities=processed_messages.modalities,
295	            lora_path=lora_path,
296	            bootstrap_host=request.bootstrap_host,
297	            bootstrap_port=request.bootstrap_port,
298	            bootstrap_room=request.bootstrap_room,
299	            data_parallel_rank=request.data_parallel_rank,
300	            return_hidden_states=request.return_hidden_states,
301	            return_routed_experts=request.return_routed_experts,
302	            rid=request.rid,
303	            extra_key=self._compute_extra_key(request),
304	            require_reasoning=self._get_reasoning_from_request(request),
305	            priority=request.priority,
306	            routing_key=self.extract_routing_key(raw_request),
307	            custom_labels=custom_labels,
308	            custom_logit_processor=request.custom_logit_processor,
309	            image_max_dynamic_patch=img_max_dynamic_patch,
310	            video_max_dynamic_patch=vid_max_dynamic_patch,
311	            max_dynamic_patch=getattr(request, "max_dynamic_patch", None),
312	        )
313	
314	        return adapted_request, request
315	
316	    def _process_messages(
317	        self, request: ChatCompletionRequest, is_multimodal: bool
318	    ) -> MessageProcessingResult:
319	        """Process chat messages and apply chat template"""
320	        # GptOss model needs to keep special tokens for harmony parsing
321	        if self.is_gpt_oss:
322	            request.skip_special_tokens = False
323	
324	        tool_call_constraint = None
325	
326	        # Apply chat template and its stop strings
327	        tools = None
328	        if request.tools and request.tool_choice != "none":
329	            request.skip_special_tokens = False
330	            if not isinstance(request.tool_choice, str):
331	                tools = [
332	                    item.model_dump()
333	                    for item in request.tools
334	                    if item.function.name == request.tool_choice.function.name
335	                ]
336	            else:
337	                tools = [item.model_dump() for item in request.tools]
338	            if self.tool_call_parser:
339	                parser = FunctionCallParser(request.tools, self.tool_call_parser)
340	                tool_call_constraint = parser.get_structure_constraint(
341	                    request.tool_choice
342	                )
343	            # Handle JSON schema constraint directly for required or named tool choice
344	            if request.tool_choice == "required" or isinstance(
345	                request.tool_choice, ToolChoice
346	            ):
347	                json_schema = get_json_schema_constraint(
348	                    request.tools, request.tool_choice
349	                )
350	                tool_call_constraint = ("json_schema", json_schema)
351	
352	        # Use chat template
353	        if self.template_manager.chat_template_name is None:
354	            result = self._apply_jinja_template(request, tools, is_multimodal)
355	        else:
356	            result = self._apply_conversation_template(request, is_multimodal)
357	
358	        result.tool_call_constraint = tool_call_constraint
359	        return result
360	
361	    def _apply_jinja_template(
362	        self,
363	        request: ChatCompletionRequest,
364	        tools: Optional[List[Dict]],
365	        is_multimodal: bool,
366	    ) -> MessageProcessingResult:
367	        """Apply Jinja chat template"""
368	        prompt = ""
369	        prompt_ids = []
370	        openai_compatible_messages = []
371	        image_data = []
372	        video_data = []
373	        audio_data = []
374	        modalities = []
375	
376	        template_content_format = self.template_manager.jinja_template_content_format
377	
378	        if self.use_dpsk_v32_encoding:
379	            thinking_mode = (
380	                "thinking"
381	                if (request.chat_template_kwargs or {}).get("thinking")
382	                else "chat"
383	            )
384	            messages = request.messages
385	            messages = [msg.model_dump() for msg in messages]
386	
387	            for msg in messages:
388	                if msg.get("content") is None:
389	                    msg["content"] = ""
390	                processed_msg = process_content_for_template_format(
391	                    msg,
392	                    template_content_format,
393	                    image_data,
394	                    video_data,
395	                    audio_data,
396	                    modalities,
397	                    use_dpsk_v32_encoding=self.use_dpsk_v32_encoding,
398	                )
399	                msg.update(processed_msg)

699	    def to_sampling_params(
700	        self,
701	        stop: List[str],
702	        model_generation_config: Dict[str, Any],
703	        tool_call_constraint: Optional[ToolCallConstraint] = None,
704	    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
705	        """
706	        Convert request to sampling parameters.
707	        Priority: user value > model generation_config > OpenAI defaults
708	        """
709	
710	        def get_param(param_name: str):
711	            value = getattr(self, param_name)
712	            if value is None:
713	                return model_generation_config.get(
714	                    param_name, self._DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS[param_name]
715	                )
716	            return value
717	
718	        # add per user request
719	        spaces_between_special_tokens = (
720	            True
721	            if self.chat_template_kwargs is None
722	            else self.chat_template_kwargs.get("spaces_between_special_tokens", True)
723	        )
724	
725	        sampling_params = {
726	            "temperature": get_param("temperature"),
727	            "max_new_tokens": self.max_completion_tokens or self.max_tokens,
728	            "min_new_tokens": self.min_tokens,
729	            "stop": stop,
730	            "stop_token_ids": self.stop_token_ids,
731	            "stop_regex": self.stop_regex,
732	            "top_p": get_param("top_p"),
733	            "top_k": get_param("top_k"),
734	            "min_p": get_param("min_p"),
735	            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
736	            "frequency_penalty": self.frequency_penalty,
737	            "repetition_penalty": get_param("repetition_penalty"),
738	            "regex": self.regex,
739	            "ebnf": self.ebnf,
740	            "n": self.n,
741	            "no_stop_trim": self.no_stop_trim,
742	            "ignore_eos": self.ignore_eos,
743	            "skip_special_tokens": self.skip_special_tokens,
744	            "logit_bias": self.logit_bias,
745	            "custom_params": self.custom_params,
746	            "sampling_seed": self.seed,
747	            "spaces_between_special_tokens": spaces_between_special_tokens,
748	        }
749	
750	        if self.response_format and self.response_format.type == "json_schema":
751	            sampling_params["json_schema"] = convert_json_schema_to_str(
752	                self.response_format.json_schema.schema_
753	            )
754	        elif self.response_format and self.response_format.type == "json_object":
755	            sampling_params["json_schema"] = '{"type": "object"}'
756	        elif self.response_format and self.response_format.type == "structural_tag":
757	            sampling_params["structural_tag"] = convert_json_schema_to_str(
758	                self.response_format.model_dump(by_alias=True)
759	            )
760	
761	        # Check if there are already existing output constraints
762	        has_existing_constraints = (
763	            sampling_params.get("regex")
764	            or sampling_params.get("ebnf")
765	            or sampling_params.get("structural_tag")
766	            or sampling_params.get("json_schema")
767	        )
768	
769	        if tool_call_constraint and has_existing_constraints:
770	            logger.warning("Constrained decoding is not compatible with tool calls.")
771	        elif tool_call_constraint:
772	            constraint_type, constraint_value = tool_call_constraint
773	            if constraint_type == "structural_tag":
774	                sampling_params[constraint_type] = convert_json_schema_to_str(
775	                    constraint_value.model_dump(by_alias=True)
776	                )
777	            elif constraint_type == "json_schema":
778	                sampling_params[constraint_type] = convert_json_schema_to_str(
779	                    constraint_value  # type: ignore
780	                )
781	            else:
782	                sampling_params[constraint_type] = constraint_value
783	
784	        return sampling_params

24	class TestJsonSchemaConstraint(unittest.TestCase):
25	    """Test JSON schema constraint generation for tool choices"""
26	
27	    def setUp(self):
28	        """Set up test tools"""
29	        self.tools = [
30	            Tool(
31	                type="function",
32	                function=Function(
33	                    name="get_weather",
34	                    description="Get weather information",
35	                    parameters={
36	                        "type": "object",
37	                        "properties": {
38	                            "location": {
39	                                "type": "string",
40	                                "description": "Location to get weather for",
41	                            },
42	                            "unit": {
43	                                "type": "string",
44	                                "enum": ["celsius", "fahrenheit"],
45	                                "description": "Temperature unit",
46	                            },
47	                        },
48	                        "required": ["location"],
49	                    },
50	                ),
51	            ),
52	            Tool(
53	                type="function",
54	                function=Function(
55	                    name="search",
56	                    description="Search for information",
57	                    parameters={
58	                        "type": "object",
59	                        "properties": {
60	                            "query": {
61	                                "type": "string",
62	                                "description": "Search query",
63	                            },
64	                        },
65	                        "required": ["query"],
66	                    },
67	                ),
68	            ),
69	        ]
70	
71	    def test_required_tool_choice_schema(self):
72	        """Test schema generation for tool_choice='required'"""
73	        schema = get_json_schema_constraint(self.tools, "required")
74	
75	        self.assertIsNotNone(schema)
76	        jsonschema.Draft202012Validator.check_schema(schema)
77	
78	        self.assertEqual(schema["type"], "array")
79	        self.assertEqual(schema["minItems"], 1)
80	        self.assertIn("items", schema)
81	        self.assertIn("anyOf", schema["items"])
82	
83	        # Should have schemas for both tools
84	        self.assertEqual(len(schema["items"]["anyOf"]), 2)
85	
86	        # Check that each tool schema is present
87	        tool_names = [
88	            item["properties"]["name"]["enum"][0] for item in schema["items"]["anyOf"]
89	        ]
90	        self.assertIn("get_weather", tool_names)
91	        self.assertIn("search", tool_names)
92	
93	    def test_specific_tool_choice_schema(self):
94	        """Test schema generation for specific tool choice"""
95	        tool_choice = ToolChoice(
96	            type="function", function=ToolChoiceFuncName(name="get_weather")
97	        )
98	        schema = get_json_schema_constraint(self.tools, tool_choice)
99	
100	        self.assertIsNotNone(schema)
101	        jsonschema.Draft202012Validator.check_schema(schema)
102	
103	        self.assertEqual(schema["type"], "array")
104	        self.assertEqual(schema["minItems"], 1)
105	        self.assertEqual(schema["maxItems"], 1)
106	
107	        # Should only have schema for the specific tool
108	        item_schema = schema["items"]
109	        self.assertEqual(item_schema["properties"]["name"]["enum"], ["get_weather"])
110	        self.assertIn("parameters", item_schema["properties"])
111	
112	    def test_specific_tool_choice_dict_schema(self):
113	        """Test schema generation for specific tool choice as ToolChoice object"""
114	        tool_choice = ToolChoice(
115	            type="function", function=ToolChoiceFuncName(name="search")
116	        )
117	        schema = get_json_schema_constraint(self.tools, tool_choice)
118

121

124	        self.assertEqual(schema["maxItems"], 1)
125

127	        item_schema = schema["items"]
128	        self.assertEqual(item_schema["properties"]["name"]["enum"], ["search"])
129	        self.assertIn("parameters", item_schema["properties"])
130	
131	    def test_nonexistent_tool_choice(self):

135	        )

20	class TestJSONModeMixin:
21	    """Mixin class containing JSON mode test methods"""
22	
23	    def test_json_mode_response(self):
24	        """Test that response_format json_object (also known as "json mode") produces valid JSON, even without a system prompt that mentions JSON."""
25	        response = self.client.chat.completions.create(
26	            model=self.model,
27	            messages=[
28	                # We are deliberately omitting "That produces JSON" or similar phrases from the assistant prompt so that we don't have misleading test results
29	                {
30	                    "role": "system",
31	                    "content": "You are a helpful AI assistant that gives a short answer.",
32	                },
33	                {"role": "user", "content": "What is the capital of Bulgaria?"},
34	            ],
35	            temperature=0,
36	            max_tokens=128,
37	            response_format={"type": "json_object"},
38	        )
39	        text = response.choices[0].message.content
40	
41	        print(f"Response ({len(text)} characters): {text}")
42	
43	        # Verify the response is valid JSON
44	        try:
45	            js_obj = json.loads(text)
46	        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
47	            self.fail(f"Response is not valid JSON. Error: {e}. Response: {text}")
48	
49	        # Verify it's actually an object (dict)
50	        self.assertIsInstance(js_obj, dict, f"Response is not a JSON object: {text}")
51	
52	    def test_json_mode_with_streaming(self):
53	        """Test that streaming with json_object response (also known as "json mode") format works correctly, even without a system prompt that mentions JSON."""
54	        stream = self.client.chat.completions.create(
55	            model=self.model,
56	            messages=[
57	                # We are deliberately omitting "That produces JSON" or similar phrases from the assistant prompt so that we don't have misleading test results
58	                {
59	                    "role": "system",
60	                    "content": "You are a helpful AI assistant that gives a short answer.",
61	                },
62	                {"role": "user", "content": "What is the capital of Bulgaria?"},
63	            ],
64	            temperature=0,
65	            max_tokens=128,
66	            response_format={"type": "json_object"},
67	            stream=True,
68	        )
69	
70	        # Collect all chunks
71	        chunks = []
72	        for chunk in stream:
73	            if chunk.choices[0].delta.content is not None:
74	                chunks.append(chunk.choices[0].delta.content)
75	        full_response = "".join(chunks)
76	
77	        print(
78	            f"Concatenated Response ({len(full_response)} characters): {full_response}"
79	        )
80	
81	        # Verify the combined response is valid JSON
82	        try:
83	            js_obj = json.loads(full_response)
84	        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
85	            self.fail(
86	                f"Streamed response is not valid JSON. Error: {e}. Response: {full_response}"
87	            )
88	
89	        self.assertIsInstance(js_obj, dict)

33	    The sampling parameters.

37	    for the documentation.
38	    """
39	
40	    def __init__(
41	        self,
42	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
43	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
44	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
45	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
46	        temperature: float = 1.0,
47	        top_p: float = 1.0,
48	        top_k: int = -1,
49	        min_p: float = 0.0,
50	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
51	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
52	        repetition_penalty: float = 1.0,
53	        min_new_tokens: int = 0,
54	        n: int = 1,
55	        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
56	        regex: Optional[str] = None,
57	        ebnf: Optional[str] = None,
58	        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
59	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
60	        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
61	        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
62	        no_stop_trim: bool = False,
63	        custom_params: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
64	        stream_interval: Optional[int] = None,
65	        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None,
66	        sampling_seed: Optional[int] = None,
67	    ) -> None:
68	        self.max_new_tokens = max_new_tokens
69	        self.stop_strs = stop
70	        if stop_token_ids:
71	            self.stop_token_ids = set(stop_token_ids)
72	        else:
73	            self.stop_token_ids = None
74	        self.stop_regex_strs = stop_regex
75	        self.temperature = temperature
76	        self.top_p = top_p
77	        self.top_k = top_k

23	def _partial_json_loads(input_str: str, flags: Allow) -> Tuple[Any, int]:
24	    """
25	    Parse incomplete or partial JSON strings commonly encountered during streaming.
26	
27	    Args:
28	        input_str (str): The potentially incomplete JSON string to parse.
29	        flags (Allow): Bitwise flags controlling what types of partial data are allowed.
30	            Common flags include:
31	            - Allow.STR: Allow partial strings (e.g., '"hello wo' -> 'hello wo')
32	            - Allow.OBJ: Allow partial objects (e.g., '{"key":' -> {'key': None})
33	            - Allow.ARR: Allow partial arrays (e.g., '[1, 2,' -> [1, 2])
34	            - Allow.ALL: Allow all types of partial data
35	
36	    Returns:
37	        Tuple[Any, int]: A tuple containing:
38	            - parsed_object: The Python object parsed from the JSON
39	            - consumed_length: Number of characters consumed from input_str
40	    """
41	    try:
42	        return (partial_json_parser.loads(input_str, flags), len(input_str))
43	    except (JSONDecodeError, IndexError) as e:
44	        msg = getattr(e, "msg", str(e))
45	        if "Extra data" in msg or "pop from empty list" in msg:
46	            start = WHITESPACE.match(input_str, 0).end()
47	            obj, end = JSONDecoder().raw_decode(input_str, start)
48	            return obj, end
49	        raise

61	    """

63	
64	    Args:
65	        tools: List of tools to process
66

68	        Dictionary of consolidated $defs from all tools
69	
70	    Raises:
71	        ValueError: If conflicting $defs are found

80	                raise ValueError(

82	                    "multiple schemas, which is not "
83	                    "supported."
84	                )
85	            else:
86	                all_defs[def_name] = def_schema
87	    return all_defs

53	def convert_json_schema_to_str(json_schema: Union[dict, str, Type[BaseModel]]) -> str:
54	    """Convert a JSON schema to a string.
55	    Parameters
56	    ----------
57	    json_schema
58	        The JSON schema.
59	    Returns
60	    -------
61	    str
62	        The JSON schema converted to a string.
63	    Raises
64	    ------
65	    ValueError
66	        If the schema is not a dictionary, a string or a Pydantic class.
67	    """
68	    if isinstance(json_schema, dict):
69	        schema_str = json.dumps(json_schema)
70	    elif isinstance(json_schema, str):
71	        schema_str = json_schema
72	    elif issubclass(json_schema, BaseModel):
73	        schema_str = json.dumps(json_schema.model_json_schema())
74	    else:
75	        raise ValueError(
76	            f"Cannot parse schema {json_schema}. The schema must be either "
77	            + "a Pydantic class, a dictionary or a string that contains the JSON "
78	            + "schema specification"
79	        )
80	    return schema_str

cat > /tmp/constraint_flow_summary.md << 'EOF'
# SGLang Constraint Flow Architecture

## Overview
This document maps how constraints (json_schema, regex, ebnf, structural_tag) flow from the HTTP/API layer through the internal request processing to the grammar backends.

## 1. API Entry Points

### HTTP OpenAI-Compatible API
**File**: `/python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/serving_chat.py` (primary)

Entry point: `OpenAIServingChat._convert_to_internal_request()`

**Key fields from HTTP request**:
- `response_format` (ResponseFormat object) - Can be:
  - `type: "json_schema"` with `json_schema.schema_`
  - `type: "json_object"` 
  - `type: "structural_tag"`
- Direct constraint fields:
  - `regex`: Optional[str]
  - `ebnf`: Optional[str]
  - `json_schema`: Optional[str] (direct SRT backend param)

### Frontend Language API (sglang.lang.api)
**File**: `/python/sglang/lang/api.py`

Public functions:
- `gen(regex=..., json_schema=...)` - Returns `SglGen` IR node
- `gen_int()`, `gen_string()` - Typed variants

These are language-level APIs that eventually call the HTTP layer.

---

## 2. Protocol Definition & Parsing

**File**: `/python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py`

### ChatCompletionRequest class (lines 250-309)
Defines incoming request parameters:
```
- json_schema: Optional[str]          # Direct SRT parameter
- regex: Optional[str]                # Direct SRT parameter
- ebnf: Optional[str]                 # Direct SRT parameter  
- response_format: Optional[ResponseFormat|StructuralTagResponseFormat]
```

### ResponseFormat class (lines 153-155)
```
type: Literal["text", "json_object", "json_schema"]
json_schema: Optional[JsonSchemaResponseFormat]
```

### Key Method: `to_sampling_params()` (lines 699-784)
**This is where HTTP constraints become SamplingParams**

Constraint conversion logic:
```python
# From response_format
if response_format.type == "json_schema":
    sampling_params["json_schema"] = convert_json_schema_to_str(
        response_format.json_schema.schema_
    )
elif response_format.type == "json_object":
    sampling_params["json_schema"] = '{"type": "object"}'
elif response_format.type == "structural_tag":
    sampling_params["structural_tag"] = convert_json_schema_to_str(
        response_format.model_dump(by_alias=True)
    )

# From tool_call_constraint (function calling)
if tool_call_constraint:
    constraint_type, constraint_value = tool_call_constraint
    sampling_params[constraint_type] = convert_json_schema_to_str(constraint_value)
```

---

## 3. SamplingParams Class

**File**: `/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py`

### Core constraint fields (lines 40-67):
```python
class SamplingParams:
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None      # JSON schema string
    regex: Optional[str] = None            # Regex pattern string
    ebnf: Optional[str] = None             # EBNF grammar string
    structural_tag: Optional[str] = None   # Structural tag config (JSON str)
```

**Validation** (verify() method, lines 105-161):
- Only ONE of {json_schema, regex, ebnf} can be set (mutually exclusive)
- structural_tag is allowed alongside others

---

## 4. Internal Request Structure

**File**: `/python/sglang/srt/managers/schedule_batch.py`

### Req class (line 512+)
This represents an individual request through the pipeline:

```python
class Req:
    sampling_params: SamplingParams    # Contains constraints
    
    # Grammar-related fields (added by GrammarManager):
    grammar: Optional[BaseGrammarObject] = None
    grammar_key: Optional[str] = None      # Caching key
    grammar_wait_ct: int = 0               # Wait counter
```

The `grammar` field is populated asynchronously by the GrammarManager.

---

## 5. Grammar Manager & Compilation

**File**: `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py`

### Main method: `process_req_with_grammar()` (lines 67-105)

Flow:
1. Check if sampling_params has constraints:
   ```python
   if (req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
       or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
       or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
       or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None):
   ```

2. Create cache key: `("json", schema_str)` | `("regex", pattern)` | etc.

3. Get or create grammar via backend:
   ```python
   value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
       key, req.require_reasoning
   )
   ```

4. If cache miss, add to `grammar_queue` for background compilation

5. Later, `get_ready_grammar_requests()` polls for completion and extracts results

---

## 6. Grammar Backend (XGrammar)

**File**: `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/xgrammar_backend.py`

### XGrammarGrammarBackend class (lines 175-320)

Dispatch methods:
```python
def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
    # Compile JSON schema into grammar
    # Special case: key_string == "$$ANY$$" uses builtin JSON grammar
    ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_json_schema(schema=key_string)
    return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, ...)

def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
    ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_regex(key_string)
    return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, ...)

def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
    ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_grammar(key_string)
    return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, ...)

def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
    # Parses key_string as JSON and compiles structural tags
    ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_structural_tag(...)
    return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, ...)
```

### XGrammarGrammar class (lines 53-167)

Grammar object that constrains token decoding:
- `accept_token(token)` - Validates token matches grammar
- `fill_vocab_mask()` - Masks logits to allowed tokens
- `is_terminated()` - Checks if grammar is satisfied
- `rollback()` - Backtracks token acceptance

---

## 7. Constraint Processing in Request Serving

**File**: `/python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/serving_chat.py`

### Method: `_process_messages()` (lines 316-359)

Handles tool_call_constraint (function calling):
```python
tool_call_constraint = None

if request.tools and request.tool_choice != "none":
    # Option 1: Use parser (if configured)
    if self.tool_call_parser:
        parser = FunctionCallParser(request.tools, self.tool_call_parser)
        tool_call_constraint = parser.get_structure_constraint(request.tool_choice)
    
    # Option 2: Direct JSON schema constraint
    if request.tool_choice == "required" or isinstance(request.tool_choice, ToolChoice):
        json_schema = get_json_schema_constraint(request.tools, request.tool_choice)
        tool_call_constraint = ("json_schema", json_schema)
```

Tool schemas are converted to JSON schema constraints via:
**File**: `/python/sglang/srt/function_call/utils.py`
- `get_json_schema_constraint()` - Generates constraint schema
- `_get_tool_schema()` - Formats individual tool schemas

---

## 8. Utility Functions

**File**: `/python/sglang/utils.py` (line 53+)

```python
def convert_json_schema_to_str(json_schema: Union[dict, str, Type[BaseModel]]) -> str:
    """Convert JSON schema (dict/str/Pydantic class) to JSON string"""
    if isinstance(json_schema, dict):
        return json.dumps(json_schema)
    elif isinstance(json_schema, str):
        return json_schema
    elif issubclass(json_schema, BaseModel):
        return json.dumps(json_schema.model_json_schema())
```

Used everywhere constraints transition between formats.

---

## 9. Constraint Flow Summary

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ HTTP Request (OpenAI-Compatible API)                        │
│ - response_format.json_schema                               │
│ - response_format.type = "json_schema"|"json_object"        │
│ - regex, ebnf (direct SRT params)                           │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
         ┌───────────▼──────────┐
         │ serving_chat.py      │
         │ _process_messages()  │  Extracts constraints
         │ to_sampling_params() │  from HTTP request
         └───────────┬──────────┘
                     │
         ┌───────────▼──────────────────┐
         │ protocol.ChatCompletionRequest│
         │ .to_sampling_params()         │  Converts response_format
         │                               │  to sampling_params fields
         └───────────┬──────────────────┘
                     │
         ┌───────────▼──────────────────┐
         │ SamplingParams               │
         │ - json_schema: str | None    │
         │ - regex: str | None          │
         │ - ebnf: str | None           │
         │ - structural_tag: str | None │
         └───────────┬──────────────────┘
                     │
         ┌───────────▼──────────────────┐
         │ schedule_batch.Req           │
         │ .sampling_params (contains   │
         │  constraint strings)         │
         └───────────┬──────────────────┘
                     │
         ┌───────────▼──────────────────┐
         │ GrammarManager               │
         │ .process_req_with_grammar()  │  Creates cache key
         │ .get_ready_grammar_requests()│  Background compilation
         └───────────┬──────────────────┘
                     │
         ┌───────────▼──────────────────┐
         │ XGrammarGrammarBackend       │
         │ .dispatch_json/regex/ebnf()  │  Compiles to grammar
         │ .dispatch_structural_tag()   │
         └───────────┬──────────────────┘
                     │
         ┌───────────▼──────────────────┐
         │ XGrammarGrammar              │
         │ .fill_vocab_mask() during    │  Constrains logits
         │  token decoding              │  during generation
         └──────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## 10. Key Test Files

1. **JSON Schema Tests**:
   - `/test/registered/function_call/test_json_schema_constraint.py`
   - Tests constraint generation for tool calling

2. **JSON Mode Tests**:
   - `/test/registered/openai_server/features/test_json_mode.py`
   - Tests `response_format={"type": "json_object"}`

3. **Integration Tests**:
   - `/python/sglang/test/kits/json_constrained_kit.py`
   - Full end-to-end JSON constraint testing

---

## 11. Important Notes

### Constraint Mutual Exclusivity
- Only ONE of {json_schema, regex, ebnf} can be set (validated in SamplingParams.verify())
- structural_tag can coexist with others in some cases

### Caching
- Compiled grammars are cached by key: `(constraint_type, constraint_value_str)`
- Reduces recompilation overhead for repeated constraints

### Tool Calling Integration
- Tool function schemas are automatically converted to JSON schema constraints
- Supports `tool_choice="required"` (any tool) or specific tool selection

### Special Cases
- `"$$ANY$$"` is a builtin token for generic JSON grammar
- `"json_object"` response_format becomes `'{"type": "object"}'` constraint
- response_format takes priority over direct constraint fields

## 2. Protocol Definition & Parsing

**File**: `/python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py`

### ChatCompletionRequest class (lines 250-309)
Defines incoming request parameters:
```
- json_schema: Optional[str]          # Direct SRT parameter
- regex: Optional[str]                # Direct SRT parameter
- ebnf: Optional[str]                 # Direct SRT parameter  
- response_format: Optional[ResponseFormat|StructuralTagResponseFormat]
```

### ResponseFormat class (lines 153-155)
```
type: Literal["text", "json_object", "json_schema"]
json_schema: Optional[JsonSchemaResponseFormat]
```

### Key Method: `to_sampling_params()` (lines 699-784)
**This is where HTTP constraints become SamplingParams**

Constraint conversion logic:
```python
# From response_format
if response_format.type == "json_schema":
    sampling_params["json_schema"] = convert_json_schema_to_str(
        response_format.json_schema.schema_
    )
elif response_format.type == "json_object":
    sampling_params["json_schema"] = '{"type": "object"}'
elif response_format.type == "structural_tag":
    sampling_params["structural_tag"] = convert_json_schema_to_str(
        response_format.model_dump(by_alias=True)
    )

# From tool_call_constraint (function calling)
if tool_call_constraint:
    constraint_type, constraint_value = tool_call_constraint
    sampling_params[constraint_type] = convert_json_schema_to_str(constraint_value)
```

---

## 3. SamplingParams Class

**File**: `/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py`

### Core constraint fields (lines 40-67):
```python
class SamplingParams:
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None      # JSON schema string
    regex: Optional[str] = None            # Regex pattern string
    ebnf: Optional[str] = None             # EBNF grammar string
    structural_tag: Optional[str] = None   # Structural tag config (JSON str)
```

**Validation** (verify() method, lines 105-161):
- Only ONE of {json_schema, regex, ebnf} can be set (mutually exclusive)
- structural_tag is allowed alongside others

---

## 4. Internal Request Structure

**File**: `/python/sglang/srt/managers/schedule_batch.py`

### Req class (line 512+)
This represents an individual request through the pipeline:

```python
class Req:
    sampling_params: SamplingParams    # Contains constraints
    
    # Grammar-related fields (added by GrammarManager):
    grammar: Optional[BaseGrammarObject] = None
    grammar_key: Optional[str] = None      # Caching key
    grammar_wait_ct: int = 0               # Wait counter
```

The `grammar` field is populated asynchronously by the GrammarManager.

---

## 5. Grammar Manager & Compilation

**File**: `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py`

### Main method: `process_req_with_grammar()` (lines 67-105)

Flow:
1. Check if sampling_params has constraints:
   ```python
   if (req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
       or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
       or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
       or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None):
   ```

2. Create cache key: `("json", schema_str)` | `("regex", pattern)` | etc.

3. Get or create grammar via backend:
   ```python
   value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
       key, req.require_reasoning
   )
   ```

4. If cache miss, add to `grammar_queue` for background compilation

5. Later, `get_ready_grammar_requests()` polls for completion and extracts results

---

## 6. Grammar Backend (XGrammar)

**File**: `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/xgrammar_backend.py`

### XGrammarGrammarBackend class (lines 175-320)

Dispatch methods:
```python
def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
    # Compile JSON schema into grammar
    # Special case: key_string == "$$ANY$$" uses builtin JSON grammar
    ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_json_schema(schema=key_string)
    return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, ...)

def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
    ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_regex(key_string)
    return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, ...)

def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
    ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_grammar(key_string)
    return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, ...)

def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
    # Parses key_string as JSON and compiles structural tags
    ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_structural_tag(...)
    return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, ...)
```

### XGrammarGrammar class (lines 53-167)

Grammar object that constrains token decoding:
- `accept_token(token)` - Validates token matches grammar
- `fill_vocab_mask()` - Masks logits to allowed tokens
- `is_terminated()` - Checks if grammar is satisfied
- `rollback()` - Backtracks token acceptance

---

## 7. Constraint Processing in Request Serving

**File**: `/python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/serving_chat.py`

### Method: `_process_messages()` (lines 316-359)

Handles tool_call_constraint (function calling):
```python
tool_call_constraint = None

if request.tools and request.tool_choice != "none":
    # Option 1: Use parser (if configured)
    if self.tool_call_parser:
        parser = FunctionCallParser(request.tools, self.tool_call_parser)
        tool_call_constraint = parser.get_structure_constraint(request.tool_choice)
    
    # Option 2: Direct JSON schema constraint
    if request.tool_choice == "required" or isinstance(request.tool_choice, ToolChoice):
        json_schema = get_json_schema_constraint(request.tools, request.tool_choice)
        tool_call_constraint = ("json_schema", json_schema)
```

Tool schemas are converted to JSON schema constraints via:
**File**: `/python/sglang/srt/function_call/utils.py`
- `get_json_schema_constraint()` - Generates constraint schema
- `_get_tool_schema()` - Formats individual tool schemas

---

## 8. Utility Functions

**File**: `/python/sglang/utils.py` (line 53+)

```python
def convert_json_schema_to_str(json_schema: Union[dict, str, Type[BaseModel]]) -> str:
    """Convert JSON schema (dict/str/Pydantic class) to JSON string"""
    if isinstance(json_schema, dict):
        return json.dumps(json_schema)
    elif isinstance(json_schema, str):
        return json_schema
    elif issubclass(json_schema, BaseModel):
        return json.dumps(json_schema.model_json_schema())
```

Used everywhere constraints transition between formats.

---

## 9. Constraint Flow Summary

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ HTTP Request (OpenAI-Compatible API)                        │
│ - response_format.json_schema                               │
│ - response_format.type = "json_schema"|"json_object"        │
│ - regex, ebnf (direct SRT params)                           │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
         ┌───────────▼──────────┐
         │ serving_chat.py      │
         │ _process_messages()  │  Extracts constraints
         │ to_sampling_params() │  from HTTP request
         └───────────┬──────────┘
                     │
         ┌───────────▼──────────────────┐
         │ protocol.ChatCompletionRequest│
         │ .to_sampling_params()         │  Converts response_format
         │                               │  to sampling_params fields
         └───────────┬──────────────────┘
                     │
         ┌───────────▼──────────────────┐
         │ SamplingParams               │
         │ - json_schema: str | None    │
         │ - regex: str | None          │
         │ - ebnf: str | None           │
         │ - structural_tag: str | None │
         └───────────┬──────────────────┘
                     │
         ┌───────────▼──────────────────┐
         │ schedule_batch.Req           │
         │ .sampling_params (contains   │
         │  constraint strings)         │
         └───────────┬──────────────────┘
                     │
         ┌───────────▼──────────────────┐
         │ GrammarManager               │
         │ .process_req_with_grammar()  │  Creates cache key
         │ .get_ready_grammar_requests()│  Background compilation
         └───────────┬──────────────────┘
                     │
         ┌───────────▼──────────────────┐
         │ XGrammarGrammarBackend       │
         │ .dispatch_json/regex/ebnf()  │  Compiles to grammar
         │ .dispatch_structural_tag()   │
         └───────────┬──────────────────┘
                     │
         ┌───────────▼──────────────────┐
         │ XGrammarGrammar              │
         │ .fill_vocab_mask() during    │  Constrains logits
         │  token decoding              │  during generation
         └──────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## 10. Key Test Files

1. **JSON Schema Tests**:
   - `/test/registered/function_call/test_json_schema_constraint.py`
   - Tests constraint generation for tool calling

2. **JSON Mode Tests**:
   - `/test/registered/openai_server/features/test_json_mode.py`
   - Tests `response_format={"type": "json_object"}`

3. **Integration Tests**:
   - `/python/sglang/test/kits/json_constrained_kit.py`
   - Full end-to-end JSON constraint testing

---

## 11. Important Notes

### Constraint Mutual Exclusivity
- Only ONE of {json_schema, regex, ebnf} can be set (validated in SamplingParams.verify())
- structural_tag can coexist with others in some cases

### Caching
- Compiled grammars are cached by key: `(constraint_type, constraint_value_str)`
- Reduces recompilation overhead for repeated constraints

### Tool Calling Integration
- Tool function schemas are automatically converted to JSON schema constraints
- Supports `tool_choice="required"` (any tool) or specific tool selection

### Special Cases
- `"$$ANY$$"` is a builtin token for generic JSON grammar
- `"json_object"` response_format becomes `'{"type": "object"}'` constraint
- response_format takes priority over direct constraint fields

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
import time
from concurrent import futures
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, List

import torch

from sglang.srt.constrained.base_grammar_backend import (
    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,
    create_grammar_backend,
)
from sglang.srt.environ import envs

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from sglang.srt.managers.io_struct import AbortReq
    from sglang.srt.managers.schedule_batch import Req
    from sglang.srt.managers.scheduler import Scheduler

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class GrammarManager:
    def __init__(self, scheduler: Scheduler):
        self.scheduler = scheduler
        self.server_args = scheduler.server_args
        self.grammar_queue: List[Req] = []
        if not self.server_args.skip_tokenizer_init:
            self.grammar_backend = create_grammar_backend(
                self.server_args,
                scheduler.tokenizer,
                scheduler.model_config.vocab_size,
                scheduler.model_config.hf_eos_token_id,
            )
        else:
            self.grammar_backend = None

        self.grammar_sync_group = scheduler.dp_tp_cpu_group
        self.grammar_sync_size = scheduler.dp_tp_group.world_size
        self.grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.first_rank
        self.is_grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.is_first_rank

        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL = envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL.get()
        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS = (
            envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS.get()
        )

    def __len__(self):
        return len(self.grammar_queue)

    def clear(self):
        if self.grammar_backend:
            self.grammar_backend.reset()

    def has_waiting_grammars(self) -> bool:
        return len(self.grammar_queue) > 0

    def abort_requests(self, recv_req: AbortReq):
        for req in self.grammar_queue:
            if recv_req.abort_all or req.rid.startswith(recv_req.rid):
                logger.debug(f"Abort grammar queue request. {req.rid=}")
                if req.grammar:
                    req.grammar.cancel()
                req.set_finish_with_abort("Aborted by AbortReq.")

    def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
        # Init grammar cache for this request
        add_to_grammar_queue = False
        if (
            req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
            or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
            or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
            or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
        ):
            if self.grammar_backend is None:
                error_msg = "Grammar-based generation (json_schema, regex, ebnf, structural_tag) is not supported when the server is launched with --grammar-backend none"
                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
            else:
                if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
                    key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
                elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
                    key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
                elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
                    key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
                elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
                    key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)

                value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
                    key, req.require_reasoning
                )
                req.grammar = value

                if not cache_hit:
                    req.grammar_key = key
                    add_to_grammar_queue = True
                else:
                    if value is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:  # We hit a cached invalid grammar.
                        error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request with cache hit: {key=}"
                        req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)

        if add_to_grammar_queue:
            self.grammar_queue.append(req)

        return add_to_grammar_queue

    def get_ready_grammar_requests(self) -> List[Req]:
        """
        Move requests whose grammar objects are ready from grammar_queue to waiting_queue.

        Rank i returns two sets ready_reqs_i, failed_reqs_i
        ready_reqs_all = all_gather(ready_reqs_i)
        failed_reqs_all = all_gather(failed_reqs_i)

        ready_reqs = intersect(ready_reqs_all)
        failed_reqs = union(failed_reqs_all)
        """
        ready_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
        failed_req_idxs: set[int] = set()

        # Poll for ready requests
        start_time = time.perf_counter()
        while time.perf_counter() - start_time < self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL:
            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
                if i in ready_req_idxs:
                    continue

                if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
                    continue

                assert isinstance(req.grammar, futures.Future), f"{req=}"
                if req.grammar.done():
                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)

            # Sleep a bit to avoid busy waiting
            time.sleep(self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL / 10)

        # Check failed requests
        for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
            if i not in ready_req_idxs:
                self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct += 1
                if (
                    self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct
                    >= self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS
                ):
                    # Timeout after max poll iterations
                    # The actual waiting time is SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS * max(SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL, GPU_forward_batch_latency)
                    failed_req_idxs.add(i)

        # Sync ready and failed requests across all ranks
        if self.grammar_sync_size == 1:
            synced_ready_req_idxs = ready_req_idxs
            synced_failed_req_idxs = failed_req_idxs
        else:
            all_gather_output = [None] * self.grammar_sync_size
            torch.distributed.all_gather_object(
                all_gather_output,
                (ready_req_idxs, failed_req_idxs),
                group=self.grammar_sync_group,
            )
            synced_ready_req_idxs = set.intersection(*[x[0] for x in all_gather_output])
            synced_failed_req_idxs = set.union(*[x[1] for x in all_gather_output])

        # Return ready requests
        return_reqs: List[Req] = []
        for i in synced_ready_req_idxs:
            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
            return_reqs.append(req)
            if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
                continue

            req.grammar = req.grammar.result()
            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, req.grammar.copy())
            if req.grammar is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:
                error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request: {req.grammar_key=}"
                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)

        # Return failed requests
        for i in synced_failed_req_idxs:
            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
            return_reqs.append(req)

            req.grammar.cancel()
            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ)
            error_msg = f"Grammar preprocessing timed out: {req.grammar_key=}"
            req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)

        # Remove finished requests from grammar_queue
        self.grammar_queue = [
            req
            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue)
            if i not in synced_ready_req_idxs and i not in synced_failed_req_idxs
        ]
        return return_reqs

288	
289	def separate_reasoning(
290	    expr: Optional[SglExpr] = None, model_type: Optional[str] = None
291	):
292	    return SglExprList([expr, SglSeparateReasoning(model_type, expr=expr)])
293

1	# Copyright 2023-2024 SGLang Team

13	# ==============================================================================
14	"""Constrained decoding with xgrammar backend."""
15	
16	import dataclasses
17	import json
18	import logging
19	from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Tuple, Union
20	
21	import torch
22	from xgrammar import (
23	    CompiledGrammar,
24	    GrammarCompiler,
25	    GrammarMatcher,
26	    StructuralTagItem,
27	    TokenizerInfo,
28	    allocate_token_bitmask,
29	)
30	
31	from sglang.srt.constrained.base_grammar_backend import (
32	    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,
33	    BaseGrammarBackend,
34	    BaseGrammarObject,
35	    GrammarStats,
36	)
37	from sglang.srt.constrained.utils import is_legacy_structural_tag
38	from sglang.srt.utils import is_hip
39	
40	_is_hip = is_hip()
41	if _is_hip:
42	    from sgl_kernel import apply_token_bitmask_inplace_cuda
43	else:
44	    from sglang.srt.constrained.triton_ops.bitmask_ops import (
45	        apply_token_bitmask_inplace_triton,
46	    )
47	
48	
49	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
50	MAX_ROLLBACK_TOKENS = 200
51	
52	
53	class XGrammarGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
54	
55	    def __init__(
56	        self,
57	        matcher: GrammarMatcher,
58	        vocab_size: int,
59	        ctx: CompiledGrammar,
60	        override_stop_tokens: Optional[Union[List[int], int]],
61	        key_string: Optional[str] = None,  # TODO (sk): for debugging, remove later
62	        grammar_stats: Optional[GrammarStats] = GrammarStats(),
63	    ) -> None:
64	        super().__init__()
65	        self.matcher = matcher
66	        self.vocab_size = vocab_size
67	        self.ctx = ctx
68	        self.override_stop_tokens = override_stop_tokens
69	        self.accepted_tokens = []
70	        self.key_string = key_string
71	        self.grammar_stats = grammar_stats
72	
73	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
74	        if not self.is_terminated():
75	            self.current_token = token
76	            accepted = self.matcher.accept_token(token)
77	            if not accepted:
78	                # log for debugging
79	                raise ValueError(
80	                    f"Tokens not accepted: {token}\n"
81	                    f"Accepted tokens: {self.accepted_tokens}\n"
82	                    f"Key string: {self.key_string}"
83	                )
84	            else:
85	                self.accepted_tokens.append(token)
86	
87	    def rollback(self, k: int):
88	        self.matcher.rollback(k)
89	        self.accepted_tokens = self.accepted_tokens[:-k]
90	
91	    def is_terminated(self):
92	        return self.matcher.is_terminated()
93	
94	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
95	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
96	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
97	        return allocate_token_bitmask(batch_size, vocab_size)
98	
99	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
100	        self.matcher.fill_next_token_bitmask(vocab_mask, idx)
101	
102	    @staticmethod
103	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
104	        return vocab_mask.to(device, non_blocking=True)
105	
106	    def apply_vocab_mask(self, logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None:
107	        if (
108	            logits.device.type == "cuda"
109	            or logits.device.type == "npu"
110	            or logits.device.type == "xpu"
111	        ):
112	            if _is_hip:
113	                apply_token_bitmask_inplace_cuda(logits, vocab_mask)
114	            else:
115	                apply_token_bitmask_inplace_triton(logits, vocab_mask)
116	        elif logits.device.type == "cpu" and self.apply_vocab_mask_cpu:
117	            self.apply_vocab_mask_cpu(logits, vocab_mask)
118	        else:
119	            raise RuntimeError(f"Unsupported device: {logits.device.type}")
120	
121	    def copy(self):
122	        matcher = GrammarMatcher(
123	            self.ctx,
124	            max_rollback_tokens=MAX_ROLLBACK_TOKENS,
125	            override_stop_tokens=self.override_stop_tokens,
126	        )
127	        return XGrammarGrammar(
128	            matcher,
129	            self.vocab_size,
130	            self.ctx,
131	            self.override_stop_tokens,
132	            self.key_string,
133	            dataclasses.replace(
134	                self.grammar_stats, is_cache_hit=True, tree_traversal_time=[]
135	            ),
136	        )
137	
138	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple[List[int], str]]:
139	        s = self.matcher.find_jump_forward_string()
140	        if s:
141	            return [], s
142	        return None
143	
144	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
145	        _, data = helper
146	        return data, -1
147	
148	    def jump_and_retokenize(
149	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
150	    ):
151	        k = 0
152	        for i, old_id in enumerate(old_output_ids):
153	            if old_id == new_output_ids[i]:
154	                k = i + 1
155	            else:
156	                break
157	
158	        # rollback to the last token that is the same
159	        if k < len(old_output_ids):
160	            self.matcher.rollback(len(old_output_ids) - k)
161	
162	        for i in range(k, len(new_output_ids)):
163	            assert self.matcher.accept_token(new_output_ids[i])
164	
165	    def __repr__(self):
166	        return f"XGrammarGrammar({self.key_string=}, {self.accepted_tokens=}, {self.current_token=})"
167	
168	
169	class TokenizerNotSupportedError(Exception):
170	    """Raised when tokenizer is not supported by XGrammar backend."""
171	
172	    pass
173	
174	
175	class XGrammarGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
176	    def __init__(
177	        self,
178	        tokenizer,
179	        vocab_size: int,
180	        model_eos_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
181	        any_whitespace: bool = True,
182	    ):
183	        super().__init__()
184	
185	        if hasattr(tokenizer, "init_xgrammar"):
186	            # For special tokenizer
187	            tokenizer_info, override_stop_tokens = tokenizer.init_xgrammar()
188	
189	            if tokenizer_info is None:
190	                # Not supported tokenizer
191	                raise TokenizerNotSupportedError(
192	                    f"Tokenizer type {type(tokenizer).__name__} is not supported by XGrammar"
193	                )
194	        else:
195	            # Create TokenizerInfo with model's EOS tokens as the authoritative stop tokens
196	            # This ensures consistency between what the model considers EOS and what XGrammar uses
197	            try:
198	                tokenizer_info = TokenizerInfo.from_huggingface(
199	                    tokenizer, vocab_size=vocab_size, stop_token_ids=model_eos_token_ids
200	                )
201	                override_stop_tokens = None
202	            except Exception as e:
203	                raise TokenizerNotSupportedError(
204	                    f"Failed to create XGrammar TokenizerInfo from tokenizer: {e}"
205	                )
206	
207	        self.grammar_compiler = GrammarCompiler(tokenizer_info=tokenizer_info)
208	        self.vocab_size = vocab_size
209	        self.override_stop_tokens = override_stop_tokens
210	        self.any_whitespace = any_whitespace
211	
212	    @staticmethod
213	    def _sanitize_structural_format(structural_format):
214	        """Recursively replace missing json_schema fields with an empty schema."""
215	        if not isinstance(structural_format, dict):
216	            return
217	
218	        fmt_type = structural_format.get("type")
219	        if fmt_type in {"json_schema", "qwen_xml_parameter"}:
220	            if structural_format.get("json_schema") is None:
221	                structural_format["json_schema"] = {}
222	
223	        if fmt_type == "tag":
224	            XGrammarGrammarBackend._sanitize_structural_format(
225	                structural_format.get("content")
226	            )
227	        elif fmt_type in {"sequence", "or"}:
228	            for element in structural_format.get("elements", []):
229	                XGrammarGrammarBackend._sanitize_structural_format(element)
230	        elif fmt_type in {"triggered_tags", "tags_with_separator"}:
231	            for tag in structural_format.get("tags", []):
232	                XGrammarGrammarBackend._sanitize_structural_format(tag)
233	
234	    @staticmethod
235	    def _sanitize_structural_tag_structures(structural_tag: Dict) -> None:
236	        for structure in structural_tag.get("structures", []):
237	            if structure.get("schema") is None:
238	                structure["schema"] = {}
239	
240	    def _from_context(
241	        self, ctx: CompiledGrammar, key_string: str, grammar_stats: GrammarStats
242	    ) -> XGrammarGrammar:
243	        matcher = GrammarMatcher(
244	            ctx,
245	            max_rollback_tokens=MAX_ROLLBACK_TOKENS,
246	            override_stop_tokens=self.override_stop_tokens,
247	        )
248	        return XGrammarGrammar(
249	            matcher,
250	            self.vocab_size,
251	            ctx,
252	            self.override_stop_tokens,
253	            key_string,
254	            grammar_stats,
255	        )
256	
257	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
258	        try:
259	            if key_string == "$$ANY$$":
260	                # Note: This builtin JSON grammar includes *all* valid JSON (including, for example, arrays at the root)
261	                ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_builtin_json_grammar()
262	            else:
263	                ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_json_schema(
264	                    schema=key_string, any_whitespace=self.any_whitespace
265	                )
266	
267	        except (RuntimeError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError, UnicodeDecodeError) as e:
268	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
269	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
270	        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="json"))
271	
272	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
273	        try:
274	            ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_grammar(key_string)
275	        except RuntimeError as e:
276	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid ebnf: {key_string=}, {e=}")
277	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
278	        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="ebnf"))
279	
280	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
281	        try:
282	            ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_regex(key_string)
283	        except RuntimeError as e:
284	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid regex: {key_string=}, {e=}")
285	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
286	        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="regex"))
287	
288	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
289	        try:
290	            # TODO(dark): it's REALLY stupid to construct object from string and decode it again
291	            structural_tag = json.loads(key_string)
292	            if is_legacy_structural_tag(structural_tag):
293	                self._sanitize_structural_tag_structures(structural_tag)
294	                tags = [
295	                    StructuralTagItem(
296	                        begin=structure["begin"],
297	                        schema=json.dumps(structure["schema"]),
298	                        end=structure["end"],
299	                    )
300	                    for structure in structural_tag["structures"]
301	                ]
302	                ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_structural_tag(
303	                    tags, structural_tag["triggers"]
304	                )
305	            else:
306	                format_dict = structural_tag.get("format")
307	                if isinstance(format_dict, dict):
308	                    self._sanitize_structural_format(format_dict)
309	                    structural_tag["format"] = format_dict
310	                    key_string = json.dumps(structural_tag)
311	                ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_structural_tag(key_string)
312	        except (RuntimeError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError) as e:
313	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid structural_tag: {key_string=}, {e=}")
314	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
315	        return self._from_context(
316	            ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="structural_tag")
317	        )
318	
319	    def reset(self):
320	        self.grammar_compiler.clear_cache()
321	
322	
323	def demo_test():
324	    from transformers import AutoConfig, AutoTokenizer
325	
326	    from sglang.test.test_utils import DEFAULT_MODEL_NAME_FOR_TEST
327	
328	    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(DEFAULT_MODEL_NAME_FOR_TEST)
329	    hf_config = AutoConfig.from_pretrained(DEFAULT_MODEL_NAME_FOR_TEST)
330	
331	    # Should use vocab size from model config
332	    vocab_size = hf_config.vocab_size
333	    eos_token_id = tokenizer.eos_token_id
334	
335	    backend = XGrammarGrammarBackend(
336	        tokenizer, vocab_size=vocab_size, model_eos_token_ids=[eos_token_id]
337	    )
338	    regex = r"hello (world|there)"
339	    grammar = backend.dispatch_regex(regex)
340	    tokens = [
341	        tokenizer.encode(t, add_special_tokens=False)[0] for t in ["hello", " world"]
342	    ]
343	
344	    # Test termination
345	    grammar.accept_token(tokens[0])  # accept "hello"
346	    grammar.accept_token(tokens[1])  # accept " world"
347	    grammar.accept_token(eos_token_id)  # accept EOS
348	    assert grammar.is_terminated()
349	
350	    # Test rollback the terminated state
351	    grammar.rollback(1)
352	    assert not grammar.is_terminated()
353	
354	
355	if __name__ == "__main__":
356	    demo_test()
357
